headlines.title: 2 dead after crash involving reckless driver, Oakland police vehicle, officials say
headlines.description: Two people died Monday when a reckless driver crashed into an Oakland police vehicle, according to officials. The crash happened at about 11 a.m. near East 12th Street and 29th Avenue, according to the Oakland Housing Authority (OHA). The housing authority did not immediately provide specifics on the two fatalities or say if anyone else was injured. An Oakland Housing Authority Police Department (OHAPD) officer initially spotted a vehicle driving recklessly in the area of East 12th Street, OHA said. The officer tried to pull the driver over, but the driver took off at a high rate of speed. The officer turned off his emergency lights and siren and did not chase after the driver, a move consistent with OHAPD policy, OHA said. Shortly thereafter, the driver crashed into an Oakland Police Department (OPD) vehicle, leaving two people dead, OHA said. The driver tried to flee on foot, but they were taken into custody by OHAPD. An investigation is ongoing. Additional information wasn’t immediately available. This is a developing story. Details may change as more information becomes available. Stay tuned for updates.

headlines.title: OpenAI files for IPO as AI investment race intensifies
headlines.description: OpenAI announced Monday afternoon that it has filed for an initial public offering of stock, just a week after its chief rival, Anthropic did the same. “We recently submitted a confidential S-1,” the ChatGPT maker said in a post on X, referring to the formal name of the filing. “We have not decided on timing yet; it may be a while because there are things we want to do that are likely easier as a private company.” The artificial intelligence startup added that “it’s a complicated set of tradeoffs and this gives us the option to go public sooner if that ends up being best.” In March, OpenAI’s valuation soared to $852 billion after it raised a fresh $122 billion in cash to fuel its ever-expanding ambitions in AI. That cash is being used to develop advanced AI models, but also the extensive physical data center infrastructure and cloud computing capacity required to run those systems. Apple Inc. 14 hours ago SPACEX Jun 3 On June 1, OpenAI’s primary rival Anthropic also filed for a public stock offering. That startup was worth $952 billion in its most recent funding round. Regardless when OpenAI and Anthropic decide to begin trading this year, they will be the second and third IPOs to follow SpaceX’s trillion-dollar offering, which is set for Friday. Elon Musk’s rocket company is also heavily involved in artificial intelligence, through its xAI business.

headlines.title: Man dead, woman injured after shooting outside Kaiser facility in San Jose
headlines.description: A man died and a woman was hospitalized Monday following a shooting outside a medical facility in South San Jose, according to police. The shooting happened outside the Kaiser Permanente facility in the area of Cottle Road and Beswick Drive. Officers responded to the facility at about 10:14 a.m. and found the man, who police said is believed to be the suspect in the shooting, and the woman each suffering from at least one gunshot wound. The man was taken to a local hospital, where he later died, police said. The woman was hospitalized in critical but stable condition. Police said the man and the woman knew each other, but the exact nature of their relationship wasn’t immediately known. Detectives were working to determine a motive for the shooting. Police said the shooting was believed to be an isolated incident, adding that there was no ongoing threat to public safety. An investigation is ongoing. This story uses functionality that may not work in our app. Click here to open the story in your web browser.

headlines.title: Apple unveils an upgraded Siri voice assistant with new AI features at its annual conference
headlines.description: Apple on Monday unveiled new artificial intelligence advances including upgrades to its Siri assistant, emphasizing a focus on privacy and day-to-day use as the iPhone maker tries to catch up to rivals when it comes to AI. Siri AI, which was introduced at the start of Apple’s annual World Wide Developers Conference, has been highly anticipated by users and developers. While Siri was launched in 2011, it fell behind other voice assistants and was derided even by Apple fans. It is the last WWDC featuring CEO Tim Cook before he turns his post over to John Ternus in September. Cook received an extended standing ovation and told the audience he is “deeply grateful to have been on this journey with you” and said “the energy around Apple platforms has never been stronger.” The conference, which drew developers from some 65 countries to Apple’s Silicon Valley headquarters, focuses on software, in contrast to the fall unveiling of the latest iPhones. Apple steps up its AI game Apple has sought to distinguish itself from its peers by stressing a privacy-centered approach and integrating AI across its devices and apps. There was no mention of AI superintelligence or companion chatbots. Rather, Apple sought to demonstrate how its AI tools can make life easier. Apple software chief Craig Federighi took some swipes at AI companies — without naming them — that seem to be “pursuing AI for the sake of AI” without clear regard for the people it is supposed to serve. At Apple, he said, “we believe that truly helpful AI should be centered around you and your needs,” which means integrating AI into the products people use every day, with a focus on privacy. The conference unveiled updates to Apple Intelligence, which was first announced in 2024. It now uses Google’s Gemini AI model to help power its features and processes users’ requests and interactions on their own device and privately on the cloud, making them inaccessible to anyone else. At the center of it is the new Siri, which Apple said is now a “much more capable assistant” that can help users find what they need and get things done across various Apple devices. For instance, it can create a menu and gather recipes from the web or from your own text messages for a World Cup viewing party and invite friends from a group chat. Siri mode on your camera, meanwhile, can tell you what you are looking at and give you relevant information, such as the nutritional details of a plate of food. Siri’s visual intelligence also works with images on your screen. For example, it can tell you whether a backpack you are thinking of getting will work as a carry-on for a flight or whether a pair of bulky hiking boots will fit inside it. Apple also announced improvements to its popular AI photo editing tools, including spatial reframing that lets you adjust how a photo is framed after it was taken — as if you had moved the camera to a better position while you were snapping the picture. A standalone Siri AI app will launch later this year, though Apple said it will not initially be available in Europe and it won’t be available in China while the company works out regulatory issues. Tim Cook’s last WWDC Cook announced his retirement in April, ending a 15-year run that saw the company’s market value soar by more than $4 trillion during an iPhone-fueled era of prosperity. Ternus has been with Apple for the past quarter century, including the past five years overseeing the engineering underlying the iPhone, iPad and Mac — a role that made him a prime candidate to succeed Cook. Ternus did not take the main stage during Monday’s event. The transition to a new CEO comes at a pivotal time for Apple. Artificial intelligence has unleashed the most upheaval within the industry since Jobs unveiled the first iPhone in 2007. Apple has gotten off to a rough start in AI after stumbling in its efforts to deliver new features built on the technology, as promised nearly two years ago. Cook called his time at Apple “the honor of a lifetime.” “I truly believe the best is still ahead.”

headlines.title: East Bay motorcyclist killed in Highway 9 crash in Santa Cruz County
headlines.description: A Martinez motorcyclist was killed Sunday in rural Santa Cruz County after his bike veered off the road and struck a tree, the California Highway Patrol said. According to the CHP, the 30-year-old man was driving his 2023 Ducati motorcycle at an unknown speed on state Highway 9 south of state Highway 35 when for reasons still under investigation, he traveled off the roadway and crashed into an embankment and a tree. The man suffered major injuries and was pronounced dead at the scene, the CHP said. It is unknown at this time if alcohol or drugs played a role in the crash, according to the CHP, but an investigation is ongoing.

headlines.title: 5 vehicles stolen from Alameda County parking garage in Oakland
headlines.description: Five vehicles were stolen from an Alameda County parking garage in Oakland Sunday morning, according to authorities. The sheriff’s office said personnel discovered that the Alameda County Parking Garage at 165 13th Street in Oakland was burglarized at 7:16 a.m., and five vehicles were stolen, including Alameda County vehicles and a sheriff’s office vehicle. One of the stolen vehicles was a rental car, the sheriff’s office said. Officials said investigators initially recovered four vehicles using automated license plate reader cameras, and they were looking for the fifth. By Sunday evening, authorities told NBC Bay Area the last outstanding vehicle was found. Sergeant Roberto Morales, Public Information Officer with the Alameda County Sheriff’s Office said, “the investigation is still going to continue.” Morales added, “We’re going to try and figure out what happened, probably set up looking into our security protocols, harden the site, see what happened, how did this happen, and trying to find out who did this, why they did this.” Morales said that the thief or theives forced entry into the gararge. Several Alameda County Supervisors told NBC Bay Area Sunday they are awaiting more information abotu this incident, but plan to look into what happened. EDITOR’S NOTE: Sheriff’s officials initially reported five sheriff’s office vehicles were stolen, but they later clarified that four of them were county vehicles and at least one was a sheriff’s office unmarked vehicle.

headlines.title: Bay Area readies for World Cup as kickoff nears
headlines.description: With the FIFA World Cup set to begin in just days, changes are already underway at Levi’s Stadium in Santa Clara. During the tournament, the home of the 49ers will be known as San Francisco Bay Area Stadium, in accordance with FIFA sponsorship rules. Signage at the stadium has already been covered as part of the transition. The change has also appeared online. Apple Maps now lists the venue as San Francisco Bay Area Stadium to help visitors attending matches during the tournament. The World Cup will be played across the United States, Mexico and Canada. The tournament begins Thursday in Mexico City, while the Bay Area’s first match is scheduled for Saturday, when Qatar faces Switzerland. Meanwhile, Team Paraguay arrived in the Bay Area over the weekend. The team landed at San Jose Mineta International Airport and will use San Jose State University as its base camp during the tournament. Paraguay’s first match is scheduled for Friday in Los Angeles against the U.S. men’s national team. NBC Bay Area’s Bob Redell has more the full report in the video above.

headlines.title: Bay Area fire officials warn fire danger is only getting worse
headlines.description: Sunday was a busy day for firefighters across the Bay Area, and fire officials said the fire danger is only getting worse. Firefighters from San Jose to Santa Rosa were chasing grass fires since Sunday morning. San Jose firefighters were able to contain a fire near Communications Hill to just under 8 acres, where four different fire starts converged into one larger grass fire. The cause is under investigation. In the North Bay, Santa Rosa fire crews quickly attacked another grass fire, and stopped it from spreading to fewer than 2 acres. With temperatures getting up into the 90s and even approaching triple digits in some areas later this week, fire agencies statewide are ramping up training and getting that equipment ready. Thom Jensen has the full report in the video above.

headlines.title: Redwood City firefighters help rescue mother duck, ducklings
headlines.description: Redwood City firefighters and several Good Samaritans helped rescue a mother duck and her nine ducklings over the weekend. Firefighters said neighbors spotted the birds crossing a street when some of the ducklings fell through a storm grate and became trapped inside. The mother duck then jumped into the storm drain in an attempt to save her babies. Neighbors called firefighters for help, and crews responded by lifting the grate and rescuing all of the ducks.

headlines.title: Incident involving suspect with a knife closes Hwy. 101 in San Jose
headlines.description: An incident involving a suspect armed with a knife prompted the temporary closure of northbound Highway 101 at Hellyer Avenue on Sunday afternoon, according to authorities. San Jose police said officers responded to the 4200 block of Dove Road for a report of a trespassing with a person with a knife. At the scene, officers found the suspect acting erratic and still armed with the knife, according to authorities. Police said the suspect was taken into custody shortly before 3:30 p.m. Officials said the incident occurred near the freeway, which led to CHP temporarily closing at least a lane of northbound Highway 101 at Hellyer Avenue.

headlines.title: San Jose fire crews put out vegetation fire near Communications Hill
headlines.description: Fire crews in San Jose put out a vegetation fire Sunday afternoon that burned over 7 acres, the San Jose Fire Department said. Four separate fires converged into one in the area of Hillsdale Avenue and Communications Hill Boulevard, the Fire Department said. The call came in at about 2:20 p.m. and was under control by 4:30 p.m. A total of about 7.63 acres burned and there were no injuries. The cause of the fire is under investigation.

headlines.title: Oakland mayor announces she is running for reelection
headlines.description: Oakland’s mayor announced Saturday she is officially running for reelection. In a message posted on social media, Barbara Lee said her work is not finished, and she wants to keep fighting for a comprehensive approach to homelessness, more affordable housing, public safety, good paying jobs, and safe, clean neighborhoods. She also outlined some of her accomplishments. Lee took credit for lowering crime rates, modeling gun violence reduction, cutting homelessness in half, and breaking ground on affordable housing units.
